source_pr.title: expression: introduce EvalContext to evaluate expression
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #49415

See descriptions in issue

### What changed and how does it work?

In this PR, we just add a new interface definition `EvalContext` and copy some needed methods from `sessionctx.Context`:

```
// EvalContext is used to evaluate an expression
type EvalContext interface {
	// GetSessionVars gets the session variables.
	GetSessionVars() *variable.SessionVars
	// Value returns the value associated with this context for key.
	Value(key fmt.Stringer) interface{}
	// IsDDLOwner checks whether this session is DDL owner.
	IsDDLOwner() bool
	// GetAdvisoryLock acquires an advisory lock (aka GET_LOCK()).
	GetAdvisoryLock(string, int64) error
	// IsUsedAdvisoryLock checks for existing locks (aka IS_USED_LOCK()).
	IsUsedAdvisoryLock(string) uint64
	// ReleaseAdvisoryLock releases an advisory lock (aka RELEASE_LOCK()).
	ReleaseAdvisoryLock(string) bool
	// ReleaseAllAdvisoryLocks releases all advisory locks that this session holds.
	ReleaseAllAdvisoryLocks() int
	// GetStore returns the store of session.
	GetStore() kv.Storage
	// GetInfoSchema returns the current infoschema
	GetInfoSchema() sessionctx.InfoschemaMetaVersion
	// GetDomainInfoSchema returns the latest information schema in domain
	GetDomainInfoSchema() sessionctx.InfoschemaMetaVersion
}
```

The `sessionctx.Context` can be seen as an extend of `EvalContext`, and `EvalContext` should only provide context in the expression evaluation phase.

TODO:

1. provide `ExprBuildContext` to provide context when building expressions.
2. Redesign methods in `EvalContext` to divide them into several levels according to the scene requirement. Also, we can have several implements of `EvalContext` to provide different levels of context.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: // As multiple threads may memorize regexp and cause data race, only the first thread
failure_signature: // As multiple threads may memorize regexp and cause data race, only the first thread
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: In this PR, we just add a new interface definition `EvalContext` and copy some needed methods from `sessionctx.Context`:.
fix_pattern: In this PR, we just add a new interface definition `EvalContext` and copy some needed methods from `sessionctx.Context`:
